characters.name: Adults

characters.name: Al Gare
characters.description: Alan "Al" Gare (born 5 November 1959 in the United Kingdom) is a bassist who appeared in the early 1997 episodes of theTeletubbiesTV series. A former member ofKing Pleasure and The Biscuit Boys, he replaced guitarist Peter Prince in 1987, who was Dixie Prince. Gare was the double bassist forKing Pleasure and The Biscuit Boysfor 14 years from 1987 to 2001, the band's 15th anniversary of its origins. As withChris Shirleyand another former memberJonny Boston, Gare only appeared in the first four episodes featuringKing Pleasure and The Biscuit Boys.

characters.name: Alex Pascall
characters.description: Alex PascallOBE (born November 1936) is one of the few adults who appears in the TV Events. He tells stories and sings songs with children such as Little Baby and Handy Hands, etc. Alex Pascall OBE talks about "The Teletubbies" 2015 Alex talks about his involvement in Teletubbies

characters.name: Andy Brown
characters.description: Andrew Kevin "Andy" Brown(Born 1970) is a character from Teletubbies. He loves to teach children about animals. He only appears in TV Events. He also only appears usually in episodes that have to do with animals. Like theothers, he loves the Teletubbies. Andy Brown.

characters.name: Baa
characters.description: BaaInformationGenderMaleSpeciesTiddlytubbyDebutBabiesVoiceTeresa Gallagher Baa is the navy blue Tiddlytubby. He is voiced byTeresa Gallagher. He was one of the first Tiddlytubbies introduced in the episodeBabies. Then from the episodeTallest Shortestuntil the end of Season 1, he was seen playing on a bouncy swing. He has a navy blue body, a sphere shaped aerial and has a darker skin tone likeDipsyandDuggle Dee. Baa always likes to play boo and enjoys sliding and bouncing in his bouncy swing.
characters.personality_section: Baa always likes to play boo and enjoys sliding and bouncing in his bouncy swing.

characters.name: Baby Sun
characters.description: The Baby Sun is the sun of Teletubbyland that shines in the sky and makes baby noises. Her face is live-action and the sun is performed byJess Smithwhen she was 9 months old. This sun giggles and laughs being excited during any Magical Event or watching the Teletubbies do funny and silly things. Some fans wonder why would the creators put the baby in the sun because the sun is a baby. Jess Smithwas the Sun Baby in the original series. In the new series, the Sun Baby is played by a girl calledBerry.

characters.name: Catherine Smith
characters.description: Catherine playing with her toy farm. Catherine Smithis a character from Teletubbies. She loves to have fun and she loves her toy farm that she plays with. She only appears in TV Events. She appears inDecorating Boxes,Trickle Painting,Catherine's Toy FarmandSpray Paint Mural. Like the otherTV Event characters, she loves theTeletubbies. She made four episode appearances.

characters.name: Chris Shirley
characters.description: Christopher “Chris” Shirley (born 1962 in the United Kingdom), professionally known as Bullmoose K. Shirley, is a musician who appeared in the early 1997 episodes of theTeletubbiesTV series. He is the only Biscuit Boy to be a part ofKing Pleasure and The Biscuit Boyssince the band was established in 1986. As with former membersAl GareandJonny Boston, Shirley only appeared in the first four episodes featuringKing Pleasure and The Biscuit Boys.

characters.name: Daa-Daa
characters.description: Daa-DaaInformationGenderMaleSpeciesTiddlytubbyDebutBabiesVoiceTeresa Gallagher Daa-Daa is the green Tiddlytubby. He was one of the first Tiddlytubbies introduced in the episodeBabies. Then in the episodeTallest Shortestuntil the end of Season 1, he was just seen in the background riding on the Tiddlynoo. He is voiced byTeresa Gallagher. He has a green body and a curly shaped aerial on his head likeLaa-Laa, except it curls in the opposite direction to Laa-Laa's aerial. He resemblesDipsy. Daa-Daa always enjoys riding on the Tiddlynoo and shouting "Daa-Daa! Vroom-Vroom!"
characters.personality_section: Daa-Daa always enjoys riding on the Tiddlynoo and shouting "Daa-Daa! Vroom-Vroom!"

characters.name: Danny McCormack
characters.description: Daniel "Danny" McCormack (born 1958 in the United Kingdom) is a pianist who appeared in the early 1997 episodes of theTeletubbiesTV series. A former member ofKing Pleasure and The Biscuit Boys, he replaced Paul Skan in 1992. He was the pianist forKing Pleasure and The Biscuit Boysfor nine years from 1992 to 2001, the band's 15th anniversary of its origins.

characters.name: David Stickman
characters.description: David Stickman, known professionally asStickman, is a character in the TV receiver video event. He likes to teach the children long words and sounds. He made three episode appearances.

characters.name: Debbie Ivens
characters.description: Debbie Ivens is a character from Teletubbies. She makes 4 episode appearances. She loves to play with children, balls and to make music with them. Like theFunny Ladyandthe others, she loves children along with the Teletubbies. The children go up-close to the camera and says hello, Debbie is then shown hiding and shushes the camera, a child says 'Debbie’s going to hide and we have got to find her, come and see!', the children run to Debbie and then she says 'You've found me! Well done!', they then play music and then Debbie sings ‘It’s time to say goodbye!', a child hugs Debbie and then they all hug Debbie and they say bye. Similiar to the inside video, a child says that they will do a finger play with Debbie, they then do the finger play and the outro of the video remains the same.

characters.name: Dipsy
characters.description: Dipsyis the second Teletubby with an antenna that refers to a dipstick because of his name. Dipsy's skin tone is slightly darker than the other Teletubbies, implying that he is black. He is jolly and likes talking to himself. He wears a white hat with black cow spots. Dipsy is 8 feet tall and is green. He calls his hat "Dipsy 'Hat" which is how a toddler would say "My Hat." Dipsy is quite stubborn, sarcastic, and contemptuous, and will sometimes refuse to go along with the other Teletubbies' group opinion. He once lost his hat butLaa-Laafound it. He is the second oldest Teletubby afterTinky-Winkyand he is quite intelligent and mature. He often hangs out withLaa-LaaandTinky Winkyand is played byJohn Simmit(from 1997 - 2003, 2007, 2009) in the original series andNick Chee Ping Kellington(from 2015 - present) in the new series.

characters.name: Funny Lady
characters.description: Funny Ladyis a recurringadultcharacter who only appeared in the original series ofTeletubbies. Portrayed byTamzin Griffin, she is a nice lady who likes to tell funny stories to children. Her best friend is a teddy bear namedTeddy. The Funny Lady has long blonde hair, green pants and shirt and red overalls. She also has red lipstick. At the beginning of all the Funny Lady segments, the children run to the camera and say hello. Then, one child will go close to the camera and  say "Come and see Tamzin playing Funny Lady!" Funny Lady sits in her big chair covering her face with her hands. Then the children arrive and say "Knock Knock Knock, can we come in?" Then Funny Lady invites the children to sit down on a big couch. She then says that she's going to be the Funny Lady, after a few seconds of silence, she makes a funny face and noise and claps her hands quickly and the children laugh. Then she will tell her story. After the story is finished, Funny Lady and the children enjoy some imaginary tea and biscuits (what cookies are called in the UK) before saying goodbye.

characters.name: Jonny Boston
characters.description: Jonathon “Jonny” Boston (born 21 March 1964 in the United Kingdom) is a tenor saxophonist who appeared in the early 1997 episodes of theTeletubbiesTV series. A former member ofKing Pleasure and The Biscuit Boys, he replaced Al Nicholls in 1994, whose tenure ran for just one year. He was one of the formerKing Pleasure and The Biscuit Boysmembers whose tenure ran for one year. He was the tenor saxophonist forKing Pleasure and The Biscuit Boysfor just one year, in 1994 and 1995. As withChris Shirleyand another former memberAl Gare, Boston only appeared in the first four episodes featuringKing Pleasure and The Biscuit Boys.

characters.name: Kids
characters.description: Kids are characters from Teletubbies. They only appear in TV Events. They love to play, explore, and learn from the adults. Like theAdults, they love the Teletubbies. Some of the children who appears on the TV Events are:

characters.name: King Pleasure and The Biscuit Boys
characters.description: King Pleasure King Pleasure and The Biscuit Boysare a British jazz band shown on the early 1997 episodes of theTeletubbiestelevision series. They love to play Nursery Rhymes for both the children and for theTeletubbies. They are in episodes that usually are titled with a nursery rhyme. They made five episode appearances.

characters.name: Laa-Laa
characters.description: Laa-Laais the third Teletubby played byNikky Smedley(from 1997 - 2003, 2007, 2009) in the original series andRebecca Hyland(from 2015 - present) in the newer series. She is yellow and has a curly antenna on her head. Her favourite thing is her giant rubber orange ball. Laa-Laa is very girly. She likes playing with her orange ball, singing, dancing ballet with the skirt and singing "Laa-Laa-Li-Laa-Li-Laa-Li-Laa" to herself, hence her name. When Laa-Laa gets frustrated, she sometimes says a rather strange word, "Bibbaly Cheese!". She is often seen looking out for the other Teletubbies. She is a silly and cute Teletubby, and most of the time she has a cheerful personality. She is as intelligent asTinky Winky,DipsyandPo. She knows where places are and likes playing with the other Teletubbies. Laa-Laa often hangs out with Po and Dipsy, and even occasionally Tinky Winky. Laa-Laa is 6 feet and 6 inches tall. She is the second smallest Teletubby, as she is a bit bigger than Po. Her antenna is a curly shape like when a vine is growing. In the new series, Laa-Laa's face is slightly brighter than in the original series. Original Series Reboot series
